title: helleborus lividus var. integrifolius
intro: Helleborus lividus var. integrifolius, also known as the Corsican hellebore, is a plant species within the Helleborus genus, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of alkaloids such as helleborin. The health benefits of Helleborus lividus var. integrifolius include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antispasmodic to relieve muscle cramps. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use is often limited due to its toxicity and the risk of overdose.
